title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam D
description: A RHCSA 10 mock exam focused on RHEL 10 administration, Flatpak, systemd timers, storage, networking, users, security, and services.

tasks: On client, set hostname to clientd.exam10.lab and map serverd.exam10.lab to 192.168.122.3.
Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.63/24, gateway 192.168.122.1, and DNS 192.168.122.3.
Create /root/d-original, hard link /root/d-hard, and symlink /root/d-soft.
Create and enable examdtimer.timer that runs every 10 minutes.
Create VG vgd10 and LV datad mounted at /mnt/datad10.
Create /var/www/html/d.html and restore its default SELinux context.
Persistently enable httpd_can_network_connect.
Configure persistent systemd journal storage.
Use server as the only chrony source and enable chronyd.
Create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions using http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
Create system Flatpak remote examdflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled.
Ensure org.rhcsa.Tools is not installed after configuring examdflatpak.
Create group teamd10, create user userd10, set password cinder9, and add the user to teamd10.
Allow %teamd10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password by using a sudoers drop-in.
Set maximum password age for userd10 to 48 days and warning period to 7 days.
Create /usr/local/bin/d-who that prints the primary group for the supplied user argument.
Write users whose shell ends with sh to /root/d-shell-users.txt.
Create gzip archive /root/d-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fstab.
Create a cron job for userd10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userd10/exam10.log every 15 minutes.
On client, configure autofs so /remoted/projects mounts server:/exports/autofs/projects.
Set the default target to multi-user.target without rebooting.
Install lsof and ensure tcpdump is removed.
hints: Make changes persistent unless a question says otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks belong to the RHCSA 10 track.
checks: connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v private_dev="$private_dev" '$2 == private_dev {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.63/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
systemctl cat examdtimer.timer | grep -Fqx 'OnCalendar=*:0/10' && systemctl is-enabled examdtimer.timer | grep -qx enabled

lv_path=/dev/vgd10/datad; mapper_path=/dev/mapper/vgd10-datad; mountpoint=/mnt/datad10; lv_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$lv_path")"; lvs "$lv_path" >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET "$mountpoint" | grep -qx "$mountpoint" && awk -v dev="$lv_path" -v mapper="$mapper_path" -v uuid="$lv_uuid" -v target="$mountpoint" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == target && $3 == "xfs" && ($1 == dev || $1 == mapper || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
getsebool httpd_can_network_connect | grep -Eq -- '--> on$' && semanage boolean -l -C | grep -E '^httpd_can_network_connect\s+\(on\s*,\s*on\)'

files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk 'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}' $files
grep -Eq '^server server iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled

awk 'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo
getent group teamd10 >/dev/null && id -nG userd10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx teamd10
grep -Eq '^/remoted[[:space:]]+/etc/auto.remoted' /etc/auto.master.d/d.autofs && systemctl is-enabled autofs | grep -qx enabled
solution_outline: Work through each question independently and verify before moving on.
task_titles: set hostname to clientd.exam10.lab and map serverd.exam10.lab to 192.168
Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.63/24, gateway 192.1
Create /root/d-original, hard link /root/d-hard, and symlink /root/d-sof
Create and enable examdtimer.timer that runs every 10 minutes
Create VG vgd10 and LV datad mounted at /mnt/datad10
Create /var/www/html/d.html and restore its default SELinux context
Persistently enable httpd_can_network_connect
Configure persistent systemd journal storage
Use server as the only chrony source and enable chronyd
Create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions using http://
Create system Flatpak remote examdflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/
Ensure org.rhcsa.Tools is not installed after configuring examdflatpak
Create group teamd10, create user userd10, set password cinder9, and add
Allow %teamd10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password by using a s
Set maximum password age for userd10 to 48 days and warning period to 7
Create /usr/local/bin/d-who that prints the primary group for the suppli
Write users whose shell ends with sh to /root/d-shell-users.txt
Create gzip archive /root/d-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fs
Create a cron job for userd10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userd10/exam10
configure autofs so /remoted/projects mounts server:/exports/autofs/proj
Set the default target to multi-user.target without rebooting
Install lsof and ensure tcpdump is removed
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.63/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
